title: Plaque Toolkit ("Frankenstein's Plaque Lab")
description:
A cross-platform desktop application for measuring bacteriophage plaque size and turbidity from Petri-dish photos. Built on the peer-reviewed Plaque Size Tool (Trofimova & Jaschke, 2021), it adds a fused PST + PlaqSeg detector with an optional ResNet-18 precision gate, a manual-correction editor, millimetre calibration, batch processing, count/PFU, and optical-density turbidity comparison, plus a standalone statistics/violin analysis package.

Licensing (see LICENSING.md): our own source code and the Light build are Apache-2.0; the Full build adds the Precise engine, which is AGPL-3.0 (built on Ultralytics YOLO) with model weights under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (NonCommercial). The repository as a whole is recorded here as AGPL-3.0.

Validation status: only the Published engine reproduces a peer-reviewed method (Trofimova & Jaschke, 2021). The in-house Current/Sensitive/Precise engines and the ResNet-18 classifier gate are validated locally by the authors on their own plates (see docs/VALIDATION_RESULTS.md), not independently or peer-reviewed. PlaqSeg is an external, pre-trained detector (OnePetri lineage), not trained here.
notes: TODO before publishing the release: add each author's ORCID and affiliation (here under "creators", or in the Zenodo form). Model weights derived from the OnePetri dataset are CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial); the archive contains a Git-LFS pointer for the .pt weights, not the binary — attach the real weights to the GitHub Release if the model must be reproducible from the archive.
